article_title: Long-Term Incidence and Predicting Factors of Cranioplasty Infection after Decompressive Craniectomy
label: Fig. 1
caption: Flow chart of participants in the retrospective cohort from 2001 to 2011.

article_title: Long-Term Incidence and Predicting Factors of Cranioplasty Infection after Decompressive Craniectomy
label: Fig. 2
caption: Kaplan-Meier event-free survival curves at 10 years for cranioplasty infection according to graft material (p=0.074) (A), and cranioplasty timing (p=0.083) (B) in 131 cranioplasty patients, and according to sterilization methods (p=0.146) (C) in 83 patients who underwent cranioplasty with a cryopreserved bone graft.

article_title: Long-Term Incidence and Predicting Factors of Cranioplasty Infection after Decompressive Craniectomy
label: Fig. 3
caption: Postoperative complications within 2 weeks after cranioplasty. EDH, SDH, NA and NC stand for epidural hematoma, subdural hematoma, not applicable and not checked, respectively. Numbers in parentheses indicate the number of patients.
